metadata.title: Interactive Architecture Map
metadata.summary: A data-driven, recruiter-friendly map of the crypto analytics and ML signal platform.
scope_notes: Documentation-only. This map does not run ingestion, training, deployment, or cloud writes.
This is an analytics and ML signal platform, not a trading bot.
Batch coverage is strongest for Binance trades, ETF indicators, macro indicators, and funding data.
MLflow, Optuna, and Registry are optional and off by default.

reference_assets.label: draw.io source
reference_assets.note: Reference wireframe used to curate this interactive map.

reference_assets.label: draw.io SVG export
reference_assets.note: Reference SVG export. The interactive page renders its own HTML/CSS map instead of embedding this as the main UI.

rows.label: Primary data flow
rows.summary: Trusted batch path from sources through validation, storage, dbt, ML, and outputs.

rows.label: Streaming branch
rows.summary: Lower-latency freshness branch that rejoins warehouse/dbt and monitoring.

control_plane.label: Operational control plane
control_plane.summary: Orchestration, runtime, identity, infrastructure, and deployment gates that support the data flow.

flows.label: All
flows.summary: Show the full platform: sources, ingestion, streaming, storage, dbt, ML/MLOps, orchestration, runtime, monitoring, and CI/CD gates.

flows.label: Batch
flows.summary: Trusted historical ingestion and validation path. Strongest 5-year backfill coverage is currently Binance trades, ETF, macro, and funding.

flows.label: Streaming
flows.summary: Lower-latency producer, Kafka/Redpanda, Flink, sink, and dead-letter path. Coverage is partial and experimental compared with batch.

flows.label: dbt
flows.summary: Warehouse transformation path from raw/curated data into staging, intermediate, marts, dashboard, monitoring, and ML-ready models.

flows.label: ML/MLOps
flows.summary: Conservative artifact-first ML workflow with explicit feature contracts. MLflow, Optuna, and Registry stay optional/off by default.

flows.label: Infra/Runtime
flows.summary: Runtime, infrastructure, identity, container image, CI/CD, and Kestra gates that keep operational work explicit and reviewable.

groups.title: Sources
groups.summary: External market, ETF, macro, funding, sentiment, and experimental context sources.

nodes.title: External Data Sources
nodes.canvasSummary: Market, ETF, macro, funding, sentiment, and context feeds.
nodes.summary: Market, derivatives, ETF, macro, funding, sentiment, and operational context feeds enter through batch collectors or streaming producers.
nodes.what_it_does: Defines the upstream data universe the platform can ingest and transform.
nodes.where_it_lives: Collector and producer code live under batch and streaming areas.
nodes.why_it_matters: Coverage quality controls which downstream dbt marts and ML features are safe to interpret.
nodes.boundary: Reliable 5-year backfill is strongest for Binance trades, ETF, macro, and funding. Other sources are partial, experimental, or not fully live-ready.

groups.title: Ingestion
groups.summary: Batch collectors, backfills, and validation controls.

nodes.title: Batch Collectors
nodes.summary: Primary historical ingestion path for trusted and experimental batch sources.
nodes.what_it_does: Collects raw source data for downstream storage, dbt, monitoring, and ML preparation.
nodes.where_it_lives: Main batch ingestion modules live under local_scripts/batch.
nodes.why_it_matters: This is the strongest historical path and the safest starting point for reviewer evaluation.
nodes.boundary: Backfills and cloud-write collectors should run only when intentionally configured.

nodes.title: Backfill Scripts
nodes.canvasSummary: Selected historical loaders for trusted sources.
nodes.summary: Historical loaders for selected trusted sources.
nodes.what_it_does: Supports intentional historical rebuilds and data coverage checks.
nodes.where_it_lives: Backfill entrypoints live under local_scripts/batch/backfill.
nodes.why_it_matters: They explain the 5-year coverage story without requiring reviewers to run expensive jobs.
nodes.boundary: Do not run backfills as part of docs work. They can be expensive and may write to cloud targets.

nodes.title: Validation Rules
nodes.summary: Rule-based validation and quality-audit specs before downstream modeling.
nodes.what_it_does: Checks schema, freshness, data-quality, and pipeline assumptions before analytics or ML use.
nodes.where_it_lives: Validation code and specs live under local_scripts/batch/validation and local_scripts/batch/quality_audit/specs.
nodes.why_it_matters: Validation is the bridge between raw ingestion and trustworthy marts/signals.
nodes.boundary: Validation docs are safe to read. Validation runs can depend on configured data targets.

groups.title: Streaming
groups.summary: Lower-latency freshness path with Kafka/Redpanda, Flink, sinks, and dead-letter handling.

nodes.title: Streaming Producers
nodes.summary: Producer layer for lower-latency market, sentiment, and context experiments.
nodes.what_it_does: Publishes event-style data for streaming transformations and freshness experiments.
nodes.where_it_lives: Producer code lives under local_scripts/streaming/producer.
nodes.why_it_matters: It demonstrates a streaming architecture path without claiming full live-readiness.
nodes.boundary: Streaming sources are partial/experimental and should not be treated as fully production-live coverage.

nodes.title: Kafka / Redpanda
nodes.summary: Broker-oriented local/runtime surface for event transport.
nodes.what_it_does: Provides the message backbone for streaming producers and transformations.
nodes.where_it_lives: Streaming compose and supporting scripts live under local_scripts/streaming.
nodes.why_it_matters: It shows how the lower-latency path would be wired without requiring cloud deployment.
nodes.boundary: This is not required for batch/dbt/ML documentation review.

nodes.title: Flink Transformations
nodes.summary: Streaming transformation logic for freshness-oriented events.
nodes.what_it_does: Transforms streaming events before they move toward sinks, alerts, or dead-letter handling.
nodes.where_it_lives: Transformation code lives under local_scripts/streaming/logic_crypto_streaming.
nodes.why_it_matters: It separates raw event capture from downstream analytics-oriented event shape.
nodes.boundary: Streaming transformations are lower-latency candidates and should be validated before ML use.

nodes.title: Dead Letter Handling
nodes.summary: Failure isolation for malformed or rejected streaming records.
nodes.what_it_does: Keeps problematic events visible without silently poisoning downstream tables.
nodes.where_it_lives: Streaming logic and sink specs support dead-letter handling.
nodes.why_it_matters: It makes failure modes reviewable in a streaming design.
nodes.boundary: Dead-letter handling documents reliability intent; it does not make partial streaming sources fully live-ready.

groups.title: Storage
groups.summary: Cloud-oriented landing and warehouse surfaces used by dbt and analytics.

nodes.title: GCS Raw / Landing
nodes.canvasSummary: Object-storage landing zone for batch outputs.
nodes.summary: Cloud-storage landing concept for raw and curated batch outputs.
nodes.what_it_does: Represents the object-storage landing zone for batch outputs and downstream loaders.
nodes.where_it_lives: Terraform bucket definitions and batch loaders describe this layer.
nodes.why_it_matters: Storage boundaries explain where cloud writes would happen when explicitly configured.
nodes.boundary: Do not write to GCS as part of documentation work.

nodes.title: Iceberg / BigLake
nodes.summary: Lakehouse-oriented path for curated storage workflows.
nodes.what_it_does: Shows how curated data can move toward BigLake/Iceberg-style storage.
nodes.where_it_lives: The loader path is local_scripts/batch/iceberg_loader.py.
nodes.why_it_matters: It demonstrates cloud-native analytics design without making it mandatory for local review.
nodes.boundary: Treat this as optional infrastructure-oriented functionality.

nodes.title: BigQuery Tables
nodes.canvasSummary: Warehouse target for dbt, monitoring, and ML.
nodes.summary: Warehouse target for streaming sinks, dbt transformations, monitoring, and dashboard marts.
nodes.what_it_does: Provides the warehouse surface consumed by dbt models and downstream analytics.
nodes.where_it_lives: Dataset definitions live in Terraform; dbt models define transformation layers.
nodes.why_it_matters: BigQuery is the central analytics surface connecting ingestion, dbt, monitoring, dashboards, and ML features.
nodes.boundary: Do not write to BigQuery during docs-only work.

groups.title: Transform
groups.summary: dbt layers that convert raw and curated inputs into reviewer-friendly marts.

nodes.title: dbt Layers
nodes.summary: Staging, intermediate, marts, dashboard, monitoring, and ML-ready model layers.
nodes.what_it_does: Normalizes sources and publishes analysis-friendly tables for dashboards, monitoring, and ML.
nodes.where_it_lives: dbt project code lives under dbt_transform/crypto_dbt.
nodes.why_it_matters: dbt is the clean transformation boundary between ingestion/storage and analytics/ML consumers.
nodes.boundary: dbt models are documentation-safe to inspect; running dbt against cloud targets is an intentional data operation.

nodes.title: dbt Marts
nodes.canvasSummary: Publishes analytics, ML, dashboard, and monitoring marts.
nodes.summary: Analytics, dashboard, monitoring, and ML marts produced for consumers.
nodes.what_it_does: Publishes the curated model outputs that reviewers can reason about.
nodes.where_it_lives: Marts live under dbt_transform/crypto_dbt/models/marts.
nodes.why_it_matters: Marts show the final shape of platform analytics and ML features.
nodes.boundary: Marts depend on source coverage; partial upstream sources should stay clearly marked.

groups.title: ML/MLOps
groups.summary: Conservative artifact-first ML workflow with optional experiment tooling.

nodes.title: Feature Contract
nodes.summary: Explicit production-style feature boundary for training and prediction.
nodes.what_it_does: Defines the conservative feature set used by production-style ML paths.
nodes.where_it_lives: The contract lives in ml/feature_list.yml and related ML helpers.
nodes.why_it_matters: It prevents research candidates from silently entering production-style predictions.
nodes.boundary: Do not change ml/feature_list.yml for documentation-only work.

nodes.title: Training Workflow
nodes.canvasSummary: Creates model artifacts when explicitly run.
nodes.summary: Training entrypoint for artifact-first model creation.
nodes.what_it_does: Trains models from curated feature inputs and writes artifacts when explicitly run.
nodes.where_it_lives: Training code lives in ml/train_model.py.
nodes.why_it_matters: It demonstrates MLOps design while keeping docs work separate from model changes.
nodes.boundary: Do not run training for this documentation task.

nodes.title: Prediction Workflow
nodes.canvasSummary: Loads approved artifacts for ML signal prediction.
nodes.summary: Artifact-first prediction path for conservative ML signals.
nodes.what_it_does: Loads approved local artifacts and produces prediction outputs when explicitly run.
nodes.where_it_lives: Prediction code lives in ml/predict_latest.py and model loading helpers.
nodes.why_it_matters: It is the clearest line between analytics/ML signals and any trading misconception.
nodes.boundary: This is not a trading bot and does not place orders.

nodes.title: Optional MLflow / Optuna / Registry
nodes.canvasSummary: Optional tracking, tuning, and registry helpers.
nodes.summary: Experiment tracking, tuning, and registry helpers that remain optional/off by default.
nodes.what_it_does: Supports experiments, tuning, and registry-style workflows when intentionally enabled.
nodes.where_it_lives: Optional helpers live in ml/mlflow_utils.py, ml/mlflow_registry.py, and ml/optuna_tuning.py.
nodes.why_it_matters: It shows MLOps awareness without making remote tracking or registry access mandatory.
nodes.boundary: MLflow, Optuna, and Registry are optional and off by default.

nodes.title: subset9 / Microstructure Candidates
nodes.summary: Research/manual feature candidates that are not production defaults.
nodes.what_it_does: Keeps exploratory feature ideas visible without promoting them into the conservative feature contract.
nodes.where_it_lives: Research work lives under ml/research and related local research paths.
nodes.why_it_matters: It prevents reviewers from confusing exploratory notebooks/scripts with production defaults.
nodes.boundary: subset9 and microstructure features are research/manual candidates, not production default features.

groups.title: Orchestration
groups.summary: Kestra flows connect raw, dbt, ML, monitoring, quality, preview, and master workflows.

nodes.title: Kestra Orchestration
nodes.summary: Workflow definitions for batch, streaming, dbt, ML, monitoring, quality, and master flows.
nodes.what_it_does: Coordinates platform jobs and makes runtime execution explicit.
nodes.where_it_lives: GKE-oriented flows live under kestra/flows-gke.
nodes.why_it_matters: Kestra is where separate engineering pieces become reviewable workflows.
nodes.boundary: Do not deploy or execute Kestra flows as part of docs work.

groups.title: Runtime / Infrastructure
groups.summary: GKE, Docker images, Workload Identity, Terraform, and CI/CD gates.

nodes.title: GKE / Kubernetes Runtime
nodes.canvasSummary: Container runtime surface for platform jobs.
nodes.summary: Containerized runtime surface for production-style batch, dbt, ML, and Kestra jobs.
nodes.what_it_does: Provides the Kubernetes execution model for reviewed jobs.
nodes.where_it_lives: Kubernetes manifests and Helm values live under k8s and helm/kestra.
nodes.why_it_matters: It shows how local code can be packaged into a cloud runtime without running it during docs review.
nodes.boundary: Do not deploy to GKE for this documentation task.

nodes.title: Docker Images / Artifact Registry
nodes.summary: Container definitions and registry infrastructure for runtime jobs.
nodes.what_it_does: Packages batch, dbt, and ML jobs into runtime images and defines registry infrastructure.
nodes.where_it_lives: Dockerfiles live under docker; registry Terraform is terraform/artifact_registry.tf.
nodes.why_it_matters: It demonstrates deployable boundaries while keeping image builds behind explicit gates.
nodes.boundary: Do not build or push images for this docs task.

nodes.title: Workload Identity / IAM
nodes.canvasSummary: Cloud identity boundary for runtime jobs.
nodes.summary: Identity boundary for cloud-facing jobs.
nodes.what_it_does: Defines how runtime jobs authenticate without embedding local secrets.
nodes.where_it_lives: IAM and Kubernetes identity pieces live under terraform and k8s.
nodes.why_it_matters: It is the security boundary between code review and cloud access.
nodes.boundary: Do not put keys, tokens, or service-account secrets into docs.

nodes.title: Terraform Infrastructure
nodes.summary: Cloud resources for datasets, buckets, registry, IAM, and runtime support.
nodes.what_it_does: Describes infrastructure without applying it during docs review.
nodes.where_it_lives: Terraform modules and resource files live under terraform and terraform-bootstrap.
nodes.why_it_matters: It makes cloud-resource intent inspectable and gated.
nodes.boundary: Do not run Terraform apply or commit state/secret tfvars.

nodes.title: CI/CD and Deployment Gates
nodes.canvasSummary: Review gates for docs, runtime, infra, and deploys.
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions and repo guardrails for docs, dbt, Kestra, Docker, Terraform, and Pages.
nodes.what_it_does: Keeps deployment and validation steps explicit and reviewable.
nodes.where_it_lives: Workflows live under .github/workflows and guard scripts under .github/scripts and scripts.
nodes.why_it_matters: It separates safe docs work from operational deploy/build actions.
nodes.boundary: This documentation task should only run repo_guard, diff checks, JSON validation, and docs build.

groups.title: Monitoring / Dashboard
groups.summary: Monitoring marts, quality flows, dashboards, and reviewer-facing outputs.

nodes.title: Monitoring and Quality
nodes.canvasSummary: Freshness, validation, and pipeline health signals.
nodes.summary: Monitoring marts and quality flows for data freshness, validation, and operational checks.
nodes.what_it_does: Surfaces data-quality and pipeline health signals for reviewers/operators.
nodes.where_it_lives: Monitoring dbt models and Kestra quality/monitoring flows live in dbt_transform and kestra.
nodes.why_it_matters: It closes the loop between ingestion, transformation, ML, and operations.
nodes.boundary: Monitoring output quality depends on upstream coverage and configured targets.

nodes.title: Dashboard / Looker / Grafana
nodes.summary: Dashboard-facing marts and optional Grafana/Looker-oriented presentation surfaces.
nodes.what_it_does: Presents curated analytics outputs for review and monitoring.
nodes.where_it_lives: Dashboard marts and Grafana dashboard definitions live under dbt_transform and terraform-grafana/dashboards.
nodes.why_it_matters: It gives reviewers a business-readable view of the transformed platform outputs.
nodes.boundary: Dashboard infra may contain local state in working directories; do not expose secrets or state in docs.

limitations: This interactive map is documentation-only and does not execute repo code.
Reliable 5-year backfill is strongest for Binance trades, ETF, macro, and funding.
Other sources are partial, experimental, or not fully live-ready.
Streaming is a freshness path, not the primary trusted historical coverage path.
MLflow, Optuna, and Registry are optional/off by default.
subset9 and microstructure features are research/manual candidates, not production defaults.
